title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Ariadne Oliver discuss the importance of timelines regarding a suspected forgery. Poirot considers the possibility that a foreign girl forged a will to become a beneficiary. During their conversation, Mrs. Oliver expresses reluctance to host guests at her London apartment, despite Poirot asking if she could accommodate two people for prudence. Mrs. Oliver also discusses her writing process, noting that she observes strangers to create characters but avoids using people she actually knows. She mentions her acquaintance, Judith Butler, and Butler's child, Miranda, noting they seem to be involved in an "interesting play."

Mrs. Oliver reveals that the document in question might not be a counterfeit. She explains that Mrs. A. Jones Smith may have legitimately included a codicil in her will leaving money to an illegitimate daughter, a document witnessed by Harriet Lymon and James Jenkins. Poirot notes that while a real document may have existed, a forged codicil is also present, which lawyers intend to challenge in court. Mrs. Oliver theorizes that a beneficiary might have forged a new codicil after the original was destroyed during a dispute with relatives.

To verify the facts, Poirot uses Judith Butler's telephone to call Mr. Fullerton, the lawyer involved at the time. Poirot discovers that the forged codicil presented at probate was not witnessed by Mrs. Lymon, but by Mary Doltie, a former employee of the Llewellyn-Smiths who has since passed away. The other witness remains James Jenkins, who moved to Australia.
